2606.06271.title: FOXGLOVE: Understanding Goal-Oriented and Anchored Writing Feedback from Experts and LLMs on Argumentative Essays
2606.06271.paper_summary: While large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate writing feedback, there remains no systematic comparison of LLM and expert feedback on the dimensions that writing research identifies as central to revision: goal-orientation, anchoring to specific sentences, and prioritization. We introduce FOXGLOVE, a dataset of 696 feedback comments written by trained writing instructors on 69 twelfth-grade argumentative essays, paired with 1,644 comments generated from four frontier LLMs under a shared protocol, totaling 2,340 comments. We provide expert quality ratings on a subset of both instructor and LLM comments. We find that instructors and LLMs distribute feedback similarly across goals and essay positions, yet instructors and models diverge on the specific sentences on which to provide feedback. Additionally, we find that models tend to write more complex feedback and use fewer questions than instructors. LLM feedback also receives higher ratings on most dimensions of quality, as rated by instructors, but much of this advantage appears to be attributable to lengthier comments. FOXGLOVE enables systematic comparison of where human and LLM feedback align, diverge, and differ.
2606.06271.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地用於生成寫作反饋，但仍然沒有對LLM和專家反饋在寫作研究所識別的修訂核心維度進行系統比較：目標導向、對特定句子的依據和優先排序。我們介紹FOXGLOVE，這是一個包含696條由受過訓練的寫作教師針對69篇十二年級論證性文章所撰寫的反饋評論的數據集，並配有根據共享協議生成的1,644條來自四個前沿LLM的評論，總計2,340條評論。我們對教師和LLM評論的子集提供專家質量評分。我們發現，教師和LLM在目標和文章位置上分配反饋的方式相似，但在具體的句子上提供反饋時，教師和模型存在分歧。此外，我們發現模型傾向於撰寫更複雜的反饋，並且使用的問題比教師少。LLM的反饋在大多數質量維度上獲得的評分也較高，這些評分由教師給出，但這種優勢在很大程度上似乎是由於評論的長度較長。FOXGLOVE使得系統比較人類和LLM反饋在何處一致、分歧和不同成為可能。
2606.06271.author: Yijun Liu et.al.
2606.06271.authors: Yijun Liu, Yifan Song, John Gallagher, Sarah Sterman, Tal August
